qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Broken authorization via has_permission() stub allows cross-organization access to all objects
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns object-level authorization bypass through ProjectMixin.has_permission() and cross-organization object IDs, not the advisory's custom_hotkeys stored-XSS script-context rendering path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via ML backend URL sends project creator auth token to attacker-controlled server
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns ML backend URL SSRF and token disclosure from ml/api_connector.py, not the advisory's custom_hotkeys persistence and unsafe rendering in templates/base.html.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a stored XSS/account-takeover chain through PATCH /api/users/{id} custom_hotkeys persistence and unsafe script-context rendering in templates/base.html via json_dumps_ensure_ascii|safe. The qualifying findings address different root causes: ProjectMixin.has_permission() authorization bypass and ML backend SSRF token leakage.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2mq9-hm29-8qch describes user.custom_hotkeys rendered in templates/base.html with json_dumps_ensure_ascii|safe, allowing </script><script> injection and browser token exfiltration from /api/current-user/token.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_b1eee38e/idor-broken-authorization/vulnerability_report.json identifies ProjectMixin.has_permission() returning True for object authorization checks.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_b1eee38e/ssrf-ml-backend-token-leak/vulnerability_report.json identifies ML backend setup() sending project.created_by.auth_token.key to an attacker-controlled URL.
The final finding manifest lists only idor-broken-authorization and ssrf-ml-backend-token-leak as qualifying_vuln findings.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so runtime tiering and PoC execution were not applicable.
